summary: Excellent soundboard capture of an epic June‑24‑1973 show highlighted by a towering Dark Star > Eyes > China Doll medley, with superb dual‑guitar work and solid recording.
review: The performance shines thanks to an extended Dark Star that flows into a high‑energy Eyes and a haunting China Doll, showcasing near‑legendary Garcia/Bobby interplay and tight rhythm section support. The soundboard source is clear and well‑balanced, though the first set is incomplete and a few lyrical flubs distract from an otherwise brilliant night.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high praise for the musical performance (especially Dark Star, Eyes, China Doll) across most reviews and a soundboard source with no major audio complaints; minor issues (missing tracks, lyrical flubs) keep it just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, well‑balanced mix with crisp instrument separation; no reported muddy or drop‑out issues. The only technical drawback is the incomplete first‑set archive.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The second‑set medley Dark Star → Eyes → China Doll creates a powerful narrative arc; the rhythm section provides percolating grooves that drive the extended jams.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar work is described as “dual‑guitar interplay” and his vocal delivery on Dark Star receives high praise.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass is highlighted for a jazzy vamp around the 6:30 mark that pulls listeners in.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir contributes phased effects and solid rhythm, noted especially during China Doll.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s organ joins near the end of Dark Star, adding tasteful color without overwhelming the jam.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill (Mickey) provides percolating rhythms and high‑hat accents that support the extended improvisations.
